citations.description: Sequential for-loop burst, not asyncio.gather concurrent burst

findings.criterion: Unit test asserts unknown-kid cooldown is concurrency-safe: a burst of concurrent requests with distinct unknown `kid`s within one cooldown window triggers exactly one JWKS fetch
findings.detail: The test `test_unknown_kid_cooldown_exactly_one_fetch` uses a sequential `for i in range(5): await v.verify_token(...)` loop, not concurrent coroutines (e.g. `asyncio.gather`). The AC requires 'concurrent requests' specifically to exercise the `asyncio.Lock` TOCTOU protection. Sequential awaits naturally serialize and never actually race on the lock, so the test does not verify the concurrency-safety property the AC demands. The count==1 observable oracle is achieved, but by sequential cooldown, not by proving the lock prevents concurrent double-fetches.
findings.title: Concurrency-safe cooldown test uses sequential awaits, not concurrent requests

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
